posts.caption:
Sahher Bambba (@sahherbambba) is exactly where she‚Äôs always wanted to be. From landing her breakthrough role as the unapologetic Karishma Talwar in The Ba**ds of Bollywood to navigating the entertainment industry as an outsider, she believes that what‚Äôs meant for her will always find its way. There‚Äôs an undeniable energy about her, and we got a glimpse of it during our latest cover shoot.
For our latest Youthquake cover, amidst LSA's official food month, Sahher joined us for a midnight adventure at a sparkly convenience store, where she was quite literally like a kid in a candy store. One moment, she‚Äôs climbing into a shopping cart; the next, she‚Äôs turning up the heat in the frozen section. Her infectious joy is impossible to miss.
Read the full cover story at the link in our bio.
Editor: Analita Seth (@analitaseth)
Shoot produced by Six Ways to Sunday (@sixwaystosundayofficial_)

Gaurav Gupta‚Äôs (@gauravguptaofficial) newest couture offering unfolds as a study in celestial romance. Rooted in the symbolism of the sun and the moon, the collection reimagines bridal dressing through the house‚Äôs signature sculptural vocabulary, where architectural drapes, fluid forms and meticulous craftsmanship converge. 

Presented in Mumbai, the showcase marks a significant moment for the designer, reaffirming India as the heart of a label that continues to shape the global conversation around couture.

#AnanyaPanday #GauravGupta

posts.caption:
With an exciting career ahead of her, when Sahher Bambba (@sahherbambba) sets her gaze on the future, she envisions some big changes, save for the hope she wishes to retain across her journey.
‚ÄúI hope that the scale of my work changes and the roles that are coming my way are even more exciting and even more challenging. I want to grow as an actor because at no point do I wish to get complacent. So the endeavour would be to just keep growing and growing, and as an actor. So I just hope that work-wise the challenges get bigger. But what I hope does not change is the curiosity and the excitement I feel every time that I step onto a set,‚Äù says our cover star.

posts.caption:
Introducing our global cover star, @TravisScott!
The Houston-born artist has spent the past year moving between worlds, crafting the follow-up to 2023‚Äôs ‚ÄòUtopia‚Äô from his Malibu studio while preparing for his much-anticipated acting debut as a bard in Christopher Nolan‚Äôs @theodysseymovie, in theaters July 17.
From music to film to fashion, Scott‚Äôs creative universe continues to expand. He also stars alongside Owen Wilson in the upcoming comedy ‚ÄòRolling Loud‚Äô and maintains long-running partnerships with @Nike and @Oakley, all while leading @cactusjack into its next chapter.
Yet at the center of it all remains the music. ‚ÄúI‚Äôve been mad inspired over the last year and a half,‚Äù Scott says. ‚ÄúI‚Äôve been buckled down and back building beats from scratch, just creating and pushing the sound.‚Äù
Read our full cover story at the link in bio as Scott opens up about the creative forces that drive him, his big-screen debut, and the importance of self-care.

posts.caption:
Nel ritrarre #ZoeKravitz nel 2025, su commissione di Anthony Vaccarello per @YSL, #FrancescoClemente ha fatto quello che fa da sempre: dipingere dal vivo, senza mai passare attraverso un‚Äôimmagine mediata. ¬´C‚Äô√® il momento dell‚Äôincontro, occhi negli occhi, che riesce a cogliere aspetti dell‚Äôinteriorit√Ý, sempre in una dimensione intermedia, che non svela mai¬ª, racconta Francesca Pietropaolo, curatrice con Robert Storr di ‚ÄúFrancesco Clemente. In Between‚Äù, fino al 6 settembre 2026 alla @TriennaleMilano: la prima mostra italiana dell‚Äôartista napoletano dopo diciassette anni. Dagli autoritratti agli acquarelli di ‚ÄúSelf-Portrait in a Better World‚Äù, fino al nomadismo indiano e alla New York di Warhol e Basquiat, Clemente costruisce un sincretismo di misticismo e colore. ¬´Non so costruire, so solo partecipare e lasciare che l‚Äôimmagine si componga da sola¬ª, spiega a @Fabia.DiDrusco sulle pagine de @LOfficielItalia, Luglio/Agosto 2026.
1. ‚ÄúPortrait of Zo√´ Kravitz, Saint Laurent Summer ‚Äò25, commissionato da Anthony Vaccarello, ¬© Francesco Clemente, foto di Argenis Apolinario, Courtesy of Saint Laurent.
2. @lofficielhommes, n.32, 2013.
4. ‚ÄúBirthday Self Portrait, 2001, ¬© Francesco Clemente, foto di Argenis Apolinario (Courtesy dell‚Äôartista e Vito Schnabel Gallery)
5. ‚ÄúWinter Flowers in Spring II‚Äù, 2025, Milk paint and ink on canvas115 1/2 x 193 inches (293.4 x 490.2 cm) overall¬©Francesco Clemente; Photo by Argenis Apolinario (Courtesy dell‚Äôartista e Vito Schnabel Gallery)
7. ‚ÄúPorta Coeli‚Äù, 1983 ¬© Francesco Clemente (Courtesy dell‚Äôartista)
8. Particolare da ‚ÄúAlba‚Äù, 2024 ¬© Francesco Clemente, photo di Argenis Apolinario (Courtesy dell‚Äôartista)
9, 10. ‚ÄúFrancesco Clemente: In Between‚Äù, Exhibition view Story of My Country I, 1990 (Foto Delfino Sisto Legnani ¬© Triennale Milano)
#Art

posts.caption:
Una voce italiana al centro del mondo. Domenica 19 luglio, al MetLife Stadium di New York, Laura Pausini sale sul palco della cerimonia di chiusura dei #FIFAWorldCup2026 al fianco di Robbie Williams e Nicole Scherzinger. Insieme cantano ¬´Desire¬ª, inno ufficiale della FIFA: quel brano che intreccia inglese e spagnolo, con l‚Äôadattamento firmato dalla stessa Pausini.
Non un debutto, un ritorno. Sullo stesso palco dove, un anno fa, aveva eseguito il brano con Williams per la prima volta.
¬´Sono orgogliosa che una voce italiana faccia parte di questa celebrazione globale¬ª, ha scritto la cantante. E in fondo √® tutto qui: musica, calcio, #Desire. La stessa emozione che unisce le persone.

The Houston-born artist has spent the past year moving between worlds, crafting the follow-up to 2023‚Äôs ‚ÄòUtopia‚Äô from his Malibu studio while preparing for his much-anticipated acting debut as a bard in Christopher Nolan‚Äôs @theodysseymovie, in theaters July 17.

From music to film to fashion, Scott‚Äôs creative universe continues to expand. He also stars alongside Owen Wilson in the upcoming comedy ‚ÄòRolling Loud‚Äô and maintains long-running partnerships with @Nike and @Oakley, all while leading @cactusjack into its next chapter.

Yet at the center of it all remains the music. ‚ÄúI‚Äôve been mad inspired over the last year and a half,‚Äù Scott says. ‚ÄúI‚Äôve been buckled down and back building beats from scratch, just creating and pushing the sound.‚Äù

Read our full cover story at the link in bio as Scott opens up about the creative forces that drive him, his big-screen debut, and the importance of self-care.

posts.caption:
The 2026 July/August issue is here!
At the center of this year‚Äôs Summer Entertainment Issue is @TravisScott, who opens up about working on the follow-up to 2023‚Äôs ‚ÄòUtopia,‚Äô the travels and experiences fueling his next chapter, and making his acting debut as part of a star-studded ensemble in Christopher Nolan‚Äôs ‚ÄòThe Odyssey.‚Äô
As much as we love a blockbuster with a stacked A-list cast, championing fresh talent is always top of mind. Inside the issue, we spotlight eight rising stars whose work this summer demands your attention, from ‚ÄòOuter Banks‚Äô star @MadisonBaileyBabe to Avantika of ‚ÄòNot Suitable for Work‚Äô to @sashacalle of ‚ÄòSugar.‚Äô
It‚Äôs also a blockbuster time for literary escapes, as the rise of #BookTok turns reading into an unexpected status symbol that‚Äôs shaping summer travel. Looking ahead to the Fall collections, we explore the age-old question of whether sex still sells, given the state of undress on the runway, red carpet, and social feeds in 2026. Stay tuned for the issue hitting newsstands this month.

posts.alt_text:
His work has brought him to countless destinations across the globe, but @TravisScott still has one major stop left on his bucket list. ‚ÄúI hit every continent on the last tour except Antarctica,‚Äù he says. ‚ÄúIt‚Äôs kind of crazy.‚Äù

For Scott, travel is about more than seeing new places‚Äîit‚Äôs about expanding perspective, and he encourages leaning into exploration whenever possible. ‚ÄúGo touch earth and go actually interact with this world,‚Äù he says. ‚ÄúIt could change your mindset.‚Äù¬Ý

Read more about Scott‚Äôs adventures at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
On the 29th anniversary of Gianni Versace‚Äôs passing, we‚Äôre revisiting eight design signatures that made him unmistakably him ‚Äî the eight commandments of @versace, if you will.
At a time when many of his peers favored austere minimalism, #GianniVersace stayed true to himself. His flashy signature looks set him apart from anyone else in the fashion world: bondage-inspired gowns, vibrant prints, and metallic miniskirts. Ever the innovator, when a technique or fabric he wanted didn‚Äôt exist, he simply invented it.
From the Medusa logo to the famed ‚Äò90s bondage collection, Gianni‚Äôs mark on the house lives on through public memory and the cyclical nature of fashion trends.
Read more on his design signatures at the link in our bio.
Photos Getty Images
#versace #gianniversacearchive #versacearchives

posts.caption:
‚ÅÝA new era for @Marni begins with a love letter to its own archive.‚ÅÝ
‚ÅÝ
@meryllrogge.personal has officially taken the helm as creative director ‚Äî and her Fall/Winter 2026 debut at Milan Fashion Week makes her mission clear: not to reproduce Marni's past, but to revive its spirit. The Belgian-born designer, an Antwerp Royal Academy alum and seven-year veteran of the Marc Jacobs studio, spent months in the house's archives before landing on silhouettes defined by natural shoulders and softly dropped waistlines ‚Äî a nod to the beloved Marni of the late '90s and early 2000s. "I've been a devotee of Marni since my teens," says Rogge. "It is the very landscape of my fashion identity."‚ÅÝ
‚ÅÝ
But it was a move to Milan, not the archives, that truly shaped her debut. "I wasn't interested in reproducing Marni's past. I wanted to revive its spirit, its emotional core," she explains. "In Belgium and Northern Europe, people are more relaxed. In Milan, people truly make an effort and dress extremely well every day." Read more from Rogge on her journey, her runway debut, and what's next for Marni ‚Äî link in bio.‚ÅÝ
‚ÅÝ
Writer Simone Vertua‚ÅÝ
‚ÅÝ
Photos courtesy of Maciej Kucia and Marni‚ÅÝ
‚ÅÝ
#Marni #MeryllRogge

posts.caption:
Why is hypersexual fashion dominating runways, red carpets, and pop culture? Welcome to fashion‚Äôs biggest contradiction.
Naked dressing‚Äîskimpy, sheer, strategically undone‚Äîhas quietly gone from scandal to formula. From Gigi Hadid‚Äôs sheer @miumiu at the Met to #Demna‚Äôs diamond-thonged @Gucci debut, this season‚Äôs biggest looks share one thread: skin as statement, not shock value.
Fashion historian @valerie_steele_fashion points to a decade-long shift, noting that hypersexual fashion ‚Äúhas become more and more normalized again in the last maybe 10 years‚Äù ‚Äî with major designers leading the charge. The proof is everywhere: @matthieu_blazy‚Äôs subversive see-through mesh @chanelofficial suit, and the bodycon, day-after dishevelment, and diamond thongs that reigned at Gucci‚Äôs Fall/Winter 2026 debut.
But here‚Äôs the twist ‚Äî as the red carpet bares more, culture is doing the opposite. Call it a sex recession, or perhaps a shift toward conservatism‚Äîbut the runway has always existed to reflect or subvert what‚Äôs going on off the runway. Read more at the link in our bio.
Writer @jessicakroy
Photos courtesy of Getty Images and HBO

posts.caption:
@Balenciaga dropped its Spring Summer 2026 preview collection in Mumbai last night, and the energy in the room felt different.
With Fatima Sana Sheikh, Bobby Deol, Mouni Roy, Sanya Malhotra, Malavika Mohanan, and Krithi Shetty in attendance, it felt less like a formal showcase and more like a real moment around fashion. The room was sharp, observant, and engaged.
Fatima Sana Shaikh said it best: ‚ÄúBalenciaga always has a chic approach and the brand is now heading towards it even more aggressively.‚Äù And that tone carried through the evening. Nothing felt loud for attention, yet everything commanded it.
What stood out was how people responded. There was curiosity, closer inspection, and genuine interest in the details. Some silhouettes felt familiar but more refined, while others brought a quiet sense of drama without trying too hard.
That is when a collection works.
Not when it demands attention, but when it earns it.
@balenciaga #Balenciaga

posts.caption:
Christian von Koenigsegg has weighed in on Ferrari‚Äôs new Manuale-by-Wire system, praising the driving enjoyment it delivers while arguing that it still can‚Äôt match the speed and engineering of Koenigsegg‚Äôs own Engage Shift System (ESS). Ferrari‚Äôs setup recreates the feel of a classic gated manual using a clutch pedal and an 8-speed dual-clutch transmission, complete with simulated stalls and traditional driver involvement, but without a direct mechanical linkage.
Koenigsegg‚Äôs ESS takes a different approach. Built around the revolutionary 9-speed Light Speed Transmission in the Koenigsegg CC850, it allows the car to function as both a true-feeling 6-speed gated manual and a fully automatic gearbox. According to Christian von Koenigsegg, the system can rev-match and execute shifts significantly faster thanks to its unique transmission architecture.
The debate isn‚Äôt really about nostalgia anymore. It‚Äôs about which manufacturer has created the ultimate modern interpretation of the manual driving experience. Ferrari is chasing emotion and authenticity, while Koenigsegg is chasing emotion and cutting-edge performance at the same time.
If you had the choice, which would you pick: Ferrari‚Äôs Manuale-by-Wire or Koenigsegg‚Äôs ESS?

posts.caption:
Dolce & Gabbana returned to Sicily with one of the most breathtaking Alta Moda presentations in the House‚Äôs history.
Staged against the spectacular backdrop of Taormina, the 2026 collection drew inspiration from Greek mythology, Sicily‚Äôs rich cultural heritage, and the timeless beauty of gods and mortals. Over 100 extraordinary couture creations showcased intricate hand embroidery, sculptural silhouettes, floral embellishments, exquisite lace, and masterful Italian craftsmanship, transforming the runway into a living work of art.
More than a fashion show, Alta Moda reaffirmed Dolce & Gabbana‚Äôs vision of couture as an immersive celebration of Italian culture, artistry, and uncompromising luxury.

posts.caption:
For the first time in FIFA World Cup history, the champions won‚Äôt just lift the iconic trophy, they‚Äôll also receive official FIFA Championship Rings.
Inspired by the tradition of American sports, the bespoke rings will be awarded to members of the winning team alongside the World Cup trophy and gold medals. Each ring will be individually numbered and custom-designed, marking a historic new chapter in football‚Äôs biggest tournament. FIFA will produce 2,026 rings in total, with 30 reserved for the champions and the remaining made available as official licensed collectibles.
The introduction of championship rings adds another symbol of victory to the world‚Äôs most prestigious sporting prize, blending football tradition with a new collectible piece of World Cup history.
#Luxurious #fifaworldcup2026

The set includes everyday tools you once carried in a pencil pouch, now remade in leather and stamped with the brand‚Äôs logo.

posts.caption:
#ManifestCover
Two years into marriage, nine into love. For Sonakshi Sinha (@aslisona) and Zaheer Iqbal (@iamzahero), togetherness has never been about grand gestures. It‚Äôs love distilled to its essentials‚Äîa shared home, an easy silence, and the same laughter that started it all.
For Manifest‚Äôs July-August cover, the couple lets us in on what ‚Äúforever‚Äù actually looks like, unguarded and unhurried. ‚ÄúI think people really tend to scare you about how your life is going to change after marriage,‚Äù says Sonakshi, adding, ‚Äúbut the beauty of it, for us at least, is that things haven‚Äôt changed at all. It still feels like I‚Äôm his girlfriend and he‚Äôs my boyfriend.‚Äù
Between banter and blessing, stillness and spontaneity, they‚Äôve found the rare thing most couples spend a lifetime chasing. A forever kind of love.
Our latest issue is now live on Magzter. Read the full cover story at the link in bio.

posts.alt_text:
#ManifestCover

Marriage didn‚Äôt change Sonakshi Sinha (@aslisona) and Zaheer Iqbal (@iamzahero). It simply gave their love a new address.

‚ÄúWe dated for seven years and knew everything about each other before marriage,‚Äù says Zaheer.

‚ÄúThat‚Äôs why we got married,‚Äù Sonakshi adds. ‚ÄúWe wanted to learn to co-exist, to live in harmony and happiness.‚Äù
Perhaps that‚Äôs what makes their story feel so effortless. Not the absence of disagreements, but the certainty that they‚Äôll always find their way back to each other.

Our July-August issue is now live on Magzter. Read the full cover story at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
For Siddharth Bavishi (@sid_bavishi), weddings transcend mere d√©cor or lavish parties. To him, they are personal milestones. His core philosophy? Guests may love the design, but what stays with them is how a celebration truly made them feel. After all, and we agree, weddings are ultimately about human emotions.
‚ÄúI‚Äôve always loved the art of bringing people together.‚Äù It‚Äôs a simple statement, but one that perfectly captures his journey from organising events to building a luxury wedding planning company, The Wedding Trunk (@weddingtrunk).
From expanding the company across India and the UAE to embracing technology, sustainability and conscious celebrations, Siddharth also shares what it really takes to build a lasting career in the business of weddings‚Äîand why the glamour seen on social media is only a fraction of the story.
Read the full interview via the link in our bio üîó
By Srishti Mukherjee (@sri.sshhhhh)
-‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî‚Äî
Manifest Approved: An interview with the people shaping the wedding industry, from designers to planners and cultural tastemakers. Each story explores the business, craft and evolving expectations behind modern weddings.

posts.caption:
Sometimes, all you need is a second to fall in love, and a lifetime to stay that way. And if a destination played Cupid here, Sri Lanka‚Äîwith its sun-soaked shores, pristine beaches and idyllic views of the coast‚Äîwould be the ultimate matchmaker.
According to WedMeGood‚Äôs Destination Wedding Report 2026, Sri Lanka has emerged as one of the fastest-growing overseas wedding spots, owing to several factors‚Äîproximity, luxury hospitality and experience-led celebrations.
We take a look at the lure that this island nation has on the wedding scene.
Read the full story via link in bioüîó

posts.caption:
The soundtrack of the Big Fat Indian Wedding is getting a global upgrade.
Luxury Indian weddings are moving beyond Bollywood medleys, with international DJs, Boiler Room-style stages, immersive live performances and festival-inspired entertainment becoming the new gold standard. From Francis Mercier‚Äôs (@francismercier) Afro-house set at a Mumbai wedding to global names like Shimza (@shimza.dj), Hugel (@hugelthug) and Kahani (@kahanimusic), couples are taking inspiration from festivals like Coachella (@coachella), Sunburn (@sunburnfestival) and Ibiza (@ibizaibiza) wedding into an experience.
And it‚Äôs not just about who‚Äôs behind the console anymore. Traditional DJ booths are being replaced by festival-style main stages, immersive lighting, premium sound systems, live drummers, saxophonists and MCs. Every detail‚Äîfrom talent scouting to technical production‚Äîis designed to deliver a world-class show.
Because in 2026, the dance floor isn‚Äôt just part of the wedding. It‚Äôs the main event.
Read more via the link in bioüîó
By Mirza Shaina Beg (@mirzashainaa)
[Techno, Technomusic, Dance, Rave]

posts.caption:
When it comes to wedding style, nothing can dethrone the good old potli bag. But this wedding season, there‚Äôs a new contender manifesting itself in mainstream wedding fashion‚Äîthe classic batua bag. Vintage, timeless and celebrity-approved, this old-fashioned coin purse is having a major revival, proving that heritage accessories never really go out of style.
From Khushi Kapoor carrying it twice during Anshula Kapoor‚Äôs pre-wedding festivities to Alia Bhatt, Sonam Kapoor and Athiya Shetty‚ÄîBollywood‚Äôs best-dressed stars are embracing the batua in every way possible.
If your wedding wardrobe is due for an accessory upgrade, it might just be time to bid adieu to potlis and say hello to batua bags. Bonus? You can carry it like a clutch or opt for one with a dainty chain string, making it as versatile as it is elegant.
Bags: Meera Mahadevia (@meera_mahadevia)
By Palak Valecha (@_palakvalecha_)

posts.caption:
Hats with words stitched across the crown in an inverted arch are ubiquitous in a certain branch of men's fashion. You can buy hats with ‚ÄúNashville‚Äù or ‚ÄúDrunk‚Äù or ‚ÄúEBITDA‚Äù  or even "Jesus" written upside down.
The capsized cap has particular purchase among pro athletes and those who track them. Steph Curry has worn a cap with ‚ÄúBay Area‚Äù inverted. The golfer Jose Luis Ballester wore an upside down ‚ÄúSun Devils‚Äù hat as he shot his way through the Masters last year. The Cowboys quarterback Dak Prescott is one of many Texas pros to put on the upside-down Dallas hat.
The hats are "more merch than fashion," our style reporter @jacobwgallagher writes in his newsletter. "Their purpose isn‚Äôt to look interesting but to shout an interest," he continues. The concept has spread far and wide, but where did the trend start? Read more at the link in our bio and subscribe to The Fashions newsletter. Photos by Noah Graham/NBAE via Getty Images, USA TODAY Sports via Reuters, Andrew Redington/Getty Images
posts.alt_text:
When most women think of Chanel ballet flats, they think of them in their prim and proper Rue Cambon element. The classic tan-and-black pair have a reputation as polished and Parisian or manicured and uptown.

But recently, a subset of creative, upwardly mobile women are traipsing around in the flats in flashy colors and funky materials. Often in physical jobs and on set for production or styling, these women wear the shoes with baggy cargos, blooming skirts or low-slung jeans, rather than the tweed skirt suits they may have been previously imagined with.

‚ÄúThey‚Äôre like a utility shoe,‚Äù Ariella Starkman, a creative strategist and producer in New York, said. ‚ÄúThey add a level of polish when I‚Äôm doing the dirty work,‚Äù she added.

Unlike luxury bags, shoes often do not appreciate in value. But Chanel flats appear to be a different story. Tap the link in our bio to read more about how Chanel flats have become the shoe du jour for fashion girls. Photos by Getty Images

posts.caption:
After a couple years of embracing tiny inseams, micro shorts and hot pants, shorts have grown long again. The trend-cycle pendulum has swung all the way back to an 1800s invention: the culotte.
The culotte is, historically and by definition, a trouser masquerading as a skirt. The style gained mainstream appeal in the late 1960s and throughout the ‚Äô70s, finding fans in Diana Ross, the model Twiggy and the fashion designer Marc Bohan, who designed a pair made from fur when he was at Christian Dior.
Recently, retailers and brands have introduced silhouettes they describe as culottes, like the long Bermuda shorts from the Gap; Bottega Veneta‚Äôs divided A-line skirts; and a trouser whose hem is slowly inching up the leg, ending mid-calf, like the ones sold by Veronica Beard.
Today‚Äôs culottes are appealing across demographics and are helping solve a number of fashion crises. Tap the link in our bio to read the history of the culotte and how the style made its return. Photos by Star Max/GC Images, via Getty Images, @simbarashecha, Dave Benett/Getty Images, Estrop/Getty Images

posts.caption:
A reader wrote to our fashion critic: "I have my mother‚Äôs satin wedding gown, worn on Oct. 25, 1941 ‚Äî just six weeks before Pearl Harbor. It‚Äôs magnificent, with long sleeves, ruching, satin buttons and a long skirt. I wore it in 1979 and had it preserved, hopefully for a future generation. That has not come to pass, and I struggle with what to do. I can‚Äôt see giving it to Salvation Army. Apparently it cannot be dyed. I‚Äôve talked to a tailor about using the skirt material to make one or two blouses. It feels as if it should go into some kind of costume museum, but it‚Äôs not a big name designer. Am I being too sentimental?"
Clothes are powerful wormholes to our memories and emotions, which is why it can be so hard to throw them away. But is there liberation in letting go of the past? At the link in our bio, read @vvfriedman's advice. Photo via Jainel Morris

posts.caption:
The summer edition of haute couture isn‚Äôt for the faint of heart. It‚Äôs always hot outside, and heading indoors seldom provides much relief. Spare a thought for the models, too: Couture looks are often bigger and more complicated than ready-to-wear clothes (the collections shown in July are for the fall season, so bigger, more complicated and heavier).
But in Dior‚Äôs actual runway show, the collection felt anything but stifling. There was a similar sensibility in the Schiaparelli collection earlier in the day, but something about the air flow at the Mus√©e Rodin put the lightness front and center. Chanel seemed equally concerned with comfort, with several looks featuring light, sheer tops. At Jean Paul Gaultier, some of the biggest couture-type creations ever sent down a runway were augmented with bare shoulders and bare backs.
See more Paris Couture Week looks from the runway and onlookers at the link in our bio. Visuals by @simbarashecha

posts.caption:
Pierpaolo Piccioli‚Äôs debut couture show for Balenciaga was a gorgeous parade of dramatic designs. The collection was recognized by many as a return to form, and while it was "beautiful, no question," as our chief fashion critic Vanessa Friedman wrote in her review, it also felt familiar.
Piccioli has embraced the extraordinary color sense that became his signature in his former job at Valentino, as well as the grace of form. He has combined that with classic Balenciaga shapes (the sack, the trapeze, the balloon skirt) made in an entirely contemporary way. On the surface, the designer is doing everything right. But is it right for right now?
Read @vvfriedman's full review of @balenciaga's Paris couture show at the link in our bio. Photos by @simbarashecha
posts.alt_text:
Hats with words stitched across the crown in an inverted arch are ubiquitous in a certain branch of men's fashion. You can buy hats with ‚ÄúNashville‚Äù or ‚ÄúDrunk‚Äù or ‚ÄúEBITDA‚Äù  or even "Jesus" written upside down.

The capsized cap has particular purchase among pro athletes and those who track them. Steph Curry has worn a cap with ‚ÄúBay Area‚Äù inverted. The golfer Jose Luis Ballester wore an upside down ‚ÄúSun Devils‚Äù hat as he shot his way through the Masters last year. The Cowboys quarterback Dak Prescott is one of many Texas pros to put on the upside-down Dallas hat.

The hats are "more merch than fashion," our style reporter @jacobwgallagher writes in his newsletter. "Their purpose isn‚Äôt to look interesting but to shout an interest," he continues. The concept has spread far and wide, but where did the trend start? Read more at the link in our bio and subscribe to The Fashions newsletter. Photos by Noah Graham/NBAE via Getty Images, USA TODAY Sports via Reuters, Andrew Redington/Getty Images

posts.caption:
#VictoriaBeckham truly is the defining #WAG. She created such a distinct aesthetic that it is still referenced today. Her husband may have been captain but she famously never wore an England #football shirt. Instead opting for slogan tank tops paired with tiny shorts or low-rise jeans, oversized sunglasses, Louboutins and a Birkin.
She knew exactly who she was and rather than dressing like a typical football fan, she dressed like Victoria Beckham: international pop star and an emerging fashion figure. 20 years later and while her style is much more refined, we will never forget the OG wag.
posts.alt_text:
The #MiddleEast has produced some of the world‚Äôs greatest designers, but why do so many come from #Lebanon? Sat at the crossroads of Silk Road trade routes linking Asia, Europe, and Africa, Lebanon developed a tradition of textiles and embroidery, with skills passed down through generations.

Couture is a huge market In the Middle East due to the social culture of weddings and events. While Haute Couture is an institutional system in Paris,  the concept has existed for centuries across India, Pakistan, the Middle East, and China. In the West, couture works as a prestige marketing tool, elsewhere this level of craftsmanship has always been part of how people dress.

Unlike heritage brands which became corporatised and part of conglomerates, Lebanon‚Äôs couture boom produced independent designers like #ZuhairMurad, #ElieSaab, and #GeorgesHobeika, who still lead their own houses. Their success built on craftsmanship, personal reputation, and loyal private clients.

#OliviaDean‚Äôs stylist truly loves her!! and when it came to dressing her biggest tour yet, @simonebeyene worked with some of the best designers and ateliers to reinterpret their collections into custom looks, capturing Olivia‚Äôs stage presence, movement, and personal style.
Some of Simone‚Äôs favourite looks include the custom M√©tiers d‚ÄôArt 2026 #Chanel skirt (slide 5) and the Spring 2026 tasselled #Ala√Øa dress (slide 2) a fitting farewell to #PieterMulier‚Äôs tenure at the house.
The tour saw beautiful couture gowns take centre stage while preserving the warmth and intimacy that have become central to @oliviadeano‚Äôs artistic identity. Simone proves that thoughtful styling can be just as powerful a storytelling tool as the music itself.
Images by @lolamansell

posts.caption:
For decades, #Chanel couture shows have ended with a bridal look, but this time #MatthieuBlazy reimagined the traditional narrative as a story of independence. Matthieu was inspired by a book of fairy tales and #GabrielleChanel‚Äôs own story, from hardship to success. A wedding dress wasn‚Äôt Chanel‚Äôs fairytale because she never got married, so instead he closed with the ‚Äúrevenge dress.‚Äù
A tribute to Chanel‚Äôs life, which was defined by ambition, freedom, and creativity rather than marriage, while famously evoking the spirit of #PrincessDiana, a symbol of confidence and reclaiming one‚Äôs own narrative.

posts.caption:
#Givenchy Spring 1997 was #AlexanderMcqueens first collection after taking over from #JohnGalliano. It actually received mixed reactions, many felt McQueen was too young and too rebellious for a house built on traditional French elegance.
His appointment represented a huge shift in fashion, as younger designers began taking control of historic couture houses. McQueen himself later criticized the collection, but over time it became one of the most important moments in his career.
#Zendaya in @givenchy Spring 1997 and accompanying @philiptreacy headpiece, styled by @luxurylaw

posts.caption:
Inside the former chapel of a stately Parisian home lies #RogerVivier‚Äôs extraordinary archive. The climate-controlled ‚Äúlaboratory‚Äù houses more than 50 years of work by one of the world‚Äôs most influential shoe designers.
@rogervivier transformed footwear, pioneering the stiletto heel and the curved ‚ÄúChoc‚Äù heel. His work for  #ChristianDior gave shoes the same level of artistry and craftsmanship as Haute Couture. Decades later, his influence continues to shape contemporary fashion, with echoes of his designs still visible at houses including #Dior, #Prada, and #SaintLaurent

posts.caption:
India's Renee Noronha has won first place in the 18-24 category at the Ironman Philippines. Completing the gruelling challenge at 15:55:37, the 20-year-old IIT Madras student upheld held her record of being the youngest Indian female Ironman finisher.
Renee battled torrential rains and unforgiving temperatures to complete the race. In conversation with SheThePeople, the triathlete also revealed that she participated on day 3 of her period, making the victory a profound reminder of her strength and resilience as a woman.
With this win, Renee has now qualified for the Ironman World Championship Hawaii, scheduled for October 2026. She is also on a mission to set a new Guinness world record to become the youngest person in the world to do a full Ironman on 6 continents. She is on track to achieve this by April 2027, at the age of 21.
Read full interview at www.shethepeople.tv. | @renee.noronha

posts.caption:
A growing number of ladies are making the first move, changing the rules of dating. A few months ago, I ran into a newly divorced friend sulking in the corner of a party. Hugs and tears followed until she asked me the all-important question: how do I start again?
My friend had a rather genuine problem; she was married to a national celebrity, and her divorce was rather public too. Since her ex was a famous figure, she discovered she had no suitors thanks to being in the public eye herself. ‚ÄúIt‚Äôs been a long and lonely year. And no one has even shown interest,‚Äù she admitted.
‚ÄúDon‚Äôt wait to be asked out, you make the first move,‚Äù I replied. She was aghast; she made a face as if I had asked her to get her tongue pierced. She said she had never asked a man out. ‚ÄúDo it once, cross that bridge, and you‚Äôll find how easy and empowering it is,‚Äù I smiled.
Visit www.shethepeople.tv to read the full opinion piece by @namratazakaria
#dating #love dating advice, relationships, dating apps, couple reels, first move, bollywood, Deepika Padukone, Queen, A suitable boy, empowerment, feminism

‚ÄúA new little life has taken root‚Äù is our interpretation of welcoming a family‚Äôs newest arrival. üå±üçÑ‚Äçüü´ 

Rather than creating a birth announcement that would be admired for a moment and forgotten, we wanted to imagine a keepsake that continues to serve a purpose, which led us to one of nature‚Äôs quietest symbols of growth.

Mushrooms emerge gently after rain, appearing only once new life has already begun beneath the forest floor. To us, they felt like a beautiful metaphor for a baby‚Äôs arrival - small at first, yet destined to fill a home with warmth, love and countless memories. Paired with handcrafted chocolates to share the family‚Äôs joy, the lamp transforms into a functional piece of d√©cor, carrying the memory of that special day.

At @theworldofnatsi, we believe every object should have a reason for being chosen, and tell a story worth keeping. Every concept is thoughtfully tailored to the occasion it celebrates. If you‚Äôre looking for a gift for a meaningful milestone, we‚Äôd love to imagine something uniquely yours. Enquire via DM. ü§é
